payload.answer: Store the full 500-row CSV as an external structured artifact (with schema and metadata) and keep durable memory minimal (schema, size, and any needed aggregates/index references). Avoid creating one durable memory card per row.
payload.decision: Store the dataset as an external/attachable structured artifact (e.g., table/blob + schema/metadata), and store only minimal summaries or indexes in durable memory. Do not persist all 500 rows as individual durable memory cards.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific named entities beyond the provided table structure and system role are provided; store guidance as policy rather than binding to a unique real-world entity.

payload.intent: Decide how the provided large CSV table (500 rows, repeating id/name/value pattern) should be stored in a personal memory system (Brain).

relationships.object: large CSV table as external structured artifact
relationships.subject: Brain

memory_cards.statement: For large tabular inputs (e.g., 500+ rows) with repeated patterns, persist the full data as a structured external artifact (table attachment) and store only derived metadata/summaries in durable memory rather than one memory card per row.

relationships.object: schema + aggregates, not all raw rows
relationships.subject: durable memory

memory_cards.statement: Persist a compact representation in durable memory: table schema (column names/types), dataset size, key uniqueness assumptions, and optional aggregates (e.g., min/max counts per id) depending on downstream needs.

relationships.object: whether to index for row-level lookup
relationships.subject: unknown user requirement

memory_cards.statement: Exact suitability of summarization vs indexing depends on whether the user needs row-level recall, lookup by id/name, or only statistical/aggregate properties; if row-level recall is required, store an index/search handle alongside the external table rather than duplicating all rows as durable cards.

details: Interpretation: large tabular data should be stored as external artifact with summarized durable metadata.

repair_options: If the user later requests exact row recall, generate an index/searchable handle for the external table and store only retrieved rows as separate durable cards on demand.
If the dataset should be queried frequently by id or name, persist additional derived indexes (e.g., mapping from id to value(s)) as compact metadata rather than raw rows.
